relations.note: Coral restriction enforces the tertiary-as-accent principle of the palette hierarchy.
Chart usage applies the blues-dominant, gold-as-emphasis ratio.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve the intended visual dominance of core brand colors.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors serve only as accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).

rules.intent: Keep headline color legible and on-brand across background contexts.
rules.rule_text: Headlines and headings use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b), switching to White on dark or gradient backgrounds.

quotes: **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure readable body copy on both light and dark surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Body text is Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds; when reversed on dark, use increased line height (line_height.light_on_dark.160 = 160%).

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Reserve gold/sunshine as an emphasis signal in data visualizations.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) are dominant; Sunshine (#faa31b) / Gold (#ffc60a) are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; gray is used for placebo.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Standardize callout background tints by callout type.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue at 70% tint (opacity.callout.light_blue_70 = 0.7) for data callouts and Golden Sand at 30% tint (opacity.callout.golden_sand_30 = 0.3) for text-heavy callouts.

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accent only; never primary CTAs, headlines, or data
effect.subject: tok_lisraya_color_tertiary_coral

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent overuse or mis-application of the Coral tertiary accent.
rules.rule_text: Coral (accent.coral.usage = #F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL] Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief explicitly calls for it.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Consistent, accessible link styling across backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Links are Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b) and underlined (body.link.decoration = underline) on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / Gold (#ffc60a) must be maintained in all instances — in the logo itself and whenever the mark is used as a pattern or graphic.
